ontology.foodon_gap_note: No specific FoodOn term exists for grilled oxtail with rice. FOODON_03302051 (beef food product) used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (Bovine meat, cooked) used as closest parent; no specific grilled oxtail composite dish code exists in FoodEx2.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT code identified for grilled oxtail with rice as a composite dish.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving is approximately 450 g, comprising roughly 200 g grilled oxtail (bone-in, edible portion ~120 g), 200 g steamed white rice, and 30 g sambal.

nutrient_source.title: Estimated composite from USDA FoodData Central: Beef, oxtails, cooked, simmered (FDC ID 174032) and Cooked white rice, long-grain (FDC ID 169756), with sambal contribution estimated from FAO/INFOODS SEA Food Composition Table
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct composite entry for Buntut Bakar with Rice exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or ICMR-NIN. Values estimated from USDA FoodData Central for cooked oxtail (FDC ID 174032) and cooked long-grain white rice (FDC ID 169756), weighted by approximate serving proportions (oxtail edible ~27%, rice ~44%, sambal ~7%, bone/inedible ~22%). Per-100g values reflect the edible composite. Sambal contribution estimated from FAO/INFOODS SEA chilli-based condiment data.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition; white rice (cooked, long-grain) GI reference
gi_source.organization: American Journal of Clinical Nutrition / University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Buntut Bakar with Rice as a composite dish. GI estimated at 56 based on the dominant glycemic contributor being cooked long-grain white rice (GI ~64–72 per University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. 2021). The oxtail and sambal components contribute negligible glycemic carbohydrates. The composite GI is moderated downward from pure rice GI (~68) to ~56 due to the fat and protein content of oxtail reducing gastric emptying rate. GL per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (56 × 46.1) / 100 ≈ 25.8 g, where available carbohydrate per 450 g serving ≈ 46.1 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content from sambal, soy sauce, and kecap manis marinade
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to reduce sodium intake, as one serving provides approximately 1395 mg sodium (310 mg per 100 g × 450 g), approaching or exceeding the recommended daily limit of 2000 mg; requesting reduced soy sauce and sambal is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load from white rice component and sweet soy sauce (kecap manis)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should be aware that the white rice component contributes a moderate glycemic load (~25.8 per serving); substituting with brown rice or reducing rice portion while increasing the oxtail protein component can help attenuate postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat and cholesterol from oxtail, and sodium from condiments
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this dish in moderation due to the saturated fat (~2.6 g per 100 g) and cholesterol (~42 mg per 100 g) from oxtail, combined with elevated sodium from soy-based marinades; grilling rather than frying reduces added fat, but portion control remains important.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Buntut Bakar with Rice is a grilled oxtail dish served with steamed white rice and sambal, originating from Indonesian cuisine and widely available in Singapore at Indonesian-style restaurants such as Dapur Penyet. 'Buntut' means oxtail in Indonesian and Malay, and 'bakar' means grilled or charcoal-grilled. The oxtail is typically marinated in a spiced mixture of sweet soy sauce (kecap manis), garlic, shallots, galangal, and lemongrass before being grilled over charcoal or a flame grill, imparting a rich, smoky, slightly caramelised exterior while the collagen-rich meat remains tender and flavourful. The dish is served alongside fragrant steamed long-grain white rice and a portion of sambal — a chilli-based condiment that adds heat and acidity. Nutritionally, the dish is a moderate-energy composite providing approximately 185 kcal per 100 g, with meaningful protein from oxtail (~10.8 g per 100 g) and carbohydrate from rice (~20.5 g per 100 g). The oxtail contributes saturated fat and cholesterol, making portion control relevant for cardiovascular health. Sodium is elevated due to soy sauce and sambal. The glycemic load is moderate, driven primarily by the white rice component. Clinically, this dish is relevant for monitoring in patients managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, and cardiovascular disease. It is halal-certified in most Singaporean Indonesian restaurants.

confidence_reason: No direct composite entry for Buntut Bakar with Rice exists in HPB Singapore Foodo, ICMR-NIN, or IMR Malaysia MyFCD. Nutrient values are estimated from USDA FoodData Central cooked oxtail and cooked white rice entries, weighted by approximate serving proportions, with sambal contribution from FAO/INFOODS SEA data. GI is an analogous estimate based on the white rice component moderated by oxtail protein and fat. Confidence is medium due to the composite estimation approach.
notes: Vitamin D (vitamin_d_mcg) is -1 as it is not reliably reported for this composite dish in the source databases used (USDA FoodData Central does not report vitamin D for cooked oxtail FDC ID 174032). All other -1 values in per_100g have been replaced with estimated numeric values where defensible data existed. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA FoodData Central component data for cooked oxtail and cooked long-grain white rice, weighted by serving proportion. The dish is classified under Meat and alternatives (not Grains and staples as in the input) because the defining culinary identity is the grilled oxtail; rice is an accompaniment. The input food_group 'Grains and staples' has been corrected to 'Meat and alternatives'. Halal status is assumed based on the restaurant context (Dapur Penyet is an Indonesian Muslim-style restaurant); this should be verified against official halal certification. Aliases consistency check performed: all strings in aliases_by_language are present verbatim in the flat aliases list.
